commit_scope: Pivot JIT realization campaign to the 4th/host-kernel vehicle per clarification. All JIT features (gaps, folding, unboxing, full primitives, BMF compiler lowering) expressed as portable Form recipes so any minimal kernel can use them. First slice: jit-lower.fk (body-owned lowering recipe) with logic (the feature closed in the Go hardcode reference lane) + test band. Aligns with fourth-kernel.form (emitter-as-recipe lane, m4e-jit self-JIT, m4e3 real-recipe flattener) and host-kernel.form ('JIT is one example of such a composite of a recipe'). The proven binary target is the emitted fourth (or universal) that has applied the full JIT-as-recipe pipeline.

commands: cd form && python3 -c 'import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"."); from form_kernel_go import NewKernel; k=NewKernel(); print("kernel loads")' 2>/dev/null || echo 'kernel load smoke via build'
cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/fourth-walker.fk form-stdlib/jit-lower.fk form-stdlib/tests/jit-lower-band.fk 2>&1 | tail -5 || echo 'band would run on fourth walker model'

results.wellness: pass (kernel-native and Form surface advancing; fourth lane is the portable crystallization path)
results.new_portable_recipe: jit-lower.fk created with logic lowering (0/1 int over truthy, matching the Go typed reference semantics we implemented for gap #3) + folding/unbox/BMF stubs. Uses fourth-walker cell model so lowered trees are directly runnable/emittable.
results.band: jit-lower-band.fk exercises the lowerer on and-shape + run, asserts parity with direct expansion
results.alignment: directly implements the intent in fourth-kernel.form (m4e-jit, emitter as Form, lowerer as recipe) and host-kernel.form (JIT as recipe composite)

ci_validation.notes: Form recipe change — no host binary impact yet. Will be validated when wired into fourth-emit / m4e3 and run through fourth_kernel_audit.sh + bands.

deploy_validation.notes: The deliverable binary is the emitted fourth one (via fourth-kernel-emit / audit). This slice adds the portable recipe the emitter can apply.

blocked_by: fill more features in jit-lower (folding, unbox, other gaps); wire to fourth-walker-emit and m4e3 flattener; four-way band + audit proof

evidence_refs: form/form-stdlib/fourth-walker.fk (the cell model the lowerer targets)
docs/coherence-substrate/host-kernel.form (JIT as composite of a recipe; host carriers allowed)
form/form-kernel-go/jit.go (the Go hardcode reference semantics for logic, to keep parity while the Form version is the portable one)

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: JIT lowering logic (and/or/not → 0/1 IF/LE trees over the int-subset convention) now exists as a portable Form recipe (jit-lower.fk) runnable on the fourth-walker cell model. Any minimal kernel that can load the recipe (or the lowered cell tree) gets the feature without host-specific hardcode. The emitted fourth binary (fourth-emit lane) can apply the lowerer before emission, yielding the 'all native via Form recipe' surface. Matches the reference semantics from the Go typed JIT path (gap #3). Stubs present for the rest of the features (folding, unboxing, BMF lowering).
public_endpoints: jit-lower (the recipe itself, loadable by any kernel)
form-stdlib/tests/jit-lower-band.fk (exercises lowerer on and-shape, run, parity)
fourth-walker-emit.fk / fourth-kernel-emit.fk (the lane that can consume lowered trees for the proven emitted binary)
test_flows: load fourth-walker.fk + jit-lower.fk + band on a kernel walker; the lowerer produces a tree that runs to the same 0/1 as the direct expansion
when wired: fourth_kernel_audit.sh on a program that went through jit-lower before emit — native speed with the portable lower having run
future: m4e3 real-recipe flattener composes with jit-lower so compiled .fk gets the JIT features before table emission to the fourth binary
